sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: ‘Uno Reverse Card this policy y’all’: Southwest changes are infuriating fans
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines is facing backlash from passengers over changes to its seating policy. Travelers are encountering difficulties finding overhead bin space, leading to frustration and calls for the airline to reverse the new rules. The situation suggests that some frequent fliers are not pleased with the recent adjustments.

feed.title: First Solar Under Scrutiny as Pomerantz Probes Potential Securities Violations
feed.summary: Pomerantz LLP is investigating First Solar for potential securities violations, following a downgrade by Jefferies which cited margin pressure, order cancellations, and profitability concerns through 2025. The solar company's stock has shown volatility, and investors are awaiting its Q4 and full-year 2025 results on February 24, 2026, for clarity on future competitiveness and how it plans to offset current financial headwinds.

feed.title: Dollar Tree Is Selling the Dreamiest $1.50 Plates for Spring, and They’re About to Fly Off Shelves
feed.summary: Dollar Tree is offering charming $1.50 floral plates, along with coordinating bowls, mugs, and glasses, perfect for spring entertaining. Shoppers are advised to purchase these popular Royal Norfolk Wild Flower Printed items quickly, as previous years have seen them sell out fast due to their affordability and appealing design. The article highlights these dishes as an easy way to refresh table decor for spring events without breaking the bank.

feed.title: CSX Is Up 15% in the Last 30 Days. Here’s How Much the Stock Could Rise in 2026
feed.summary: CSX stock has risen 15% in the last 30 days due to positive investor reaction to fourth-quarter earnings and optimistic 2026 guidance, shifting focus from short-term revenue pressure to future margin expansion. The company projects low single-digit revenue growth and significant operating margin expansion for 2026, alongside robust free cash flow growth. A valuation model suggests a target price of $49, representing a 20% upside over approximately 2.9 years, indicating the stock is undervalued based on operating leverage and disciplined capital allocation.

feed.title: How Itron (ITRI) Will Use AI To Reduce Wildfire Risks
feed.summary: Itron, Inc. (NASDAQ: ITRI) is collaborating with Toumetis on a project in Southern California to use AI for enhanced power reliability and wildfire risk reduction. This initiative, part of the EPIC program with San Diego Gas & Electric (SDG&E), integrates Toumetis' predictive analytics with Itron's smart grid data. The system aims to quickly restore power during outages, prevent future outages by identifying electrical conditions that could lead to fires, and improve operational awareness in high wildfire-risk areas.

feed.title: Eaton (ETN) is Still Buyable, Says Jim Cramer
feed.summary: Jim Cramer recommends Eaton Corporation (ETN) as a buy, citing strong fourth-quarter earnings, robust backlog, and exceeding order expectations, which suggest sustained growth. RBC Capital and Morgan Stanley both raised their price targets for ETN, maintaining Outperform and Overweight ratings, respectively. Cramer linked ETN's potential to the impressive orders seen by electrical equipment provider Vertiv, reinforcing his positive outlook on the stock.

feed.title: Corning (GLW) Is Still Buyable, Says Jim Cramer
feed.summary: Jim Cramer believes Corning (GLW) remains a buy, citing its potential to replace copper with glass in data centers and its multi-year deal with Meta. The company's shares have seen significant growth, and UBS recently raised its price target to $125 with a "Buy" rating. Cramer's positive outlook is reinforced by Corning's strong financial performance and its connections to thriving data center equipment providers.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Discusses Broadcom (AVGO) Stock
feed.summary: Jim Cramer has repeatedly praised Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) and its CEO Hock Tan, calling him a top executive. Financial institutions like Jefferies and UBS have maintained Buy ratings on the stock, citing Broadcom's potential benefits from its presence in the AI space and the growing demand for tensor processing units. Cramer still believes the stock should have performed even better given its strong position.

feed.title: What is streamflation? (and will prices keep rising?)
feed.summary: Streaming service prices, dubbed "streamflation," are rising significantly faster than general inflation, with a 19.5% increase in the "subscription and rental of video and video games" category. This surge is attributed to streaming companies shifting focus from subscriber growth to profitability and sustainability, coupled with escalating content costs, particularly for sports rights. While a 29% increase was seen in the 12 months ending December 2025, experts predict future increases will be more incremental, and subscribers retain the flexibility to downgrade or cancel services.

feed.title: Is Sprouts Farmers Market (SFM) Still Attractive After Recent Share Price Pullback?
feed.summary: This article analyzes whether Sprouts Farmers Market (SFM) is attractive after a recent share price pullback. Using a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis, the stock is estimated to be 75.1% undervalued, with an intrinsic value of US$277.37 per share against a current price of US$68.96. The Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio also suggests the stock is undervalued compared to industry averages and Simply Wall St's Fair Ratio, making a case for potential investor interest despite recent declines.

feed.title: How Pebblebrook’s Debt Refinancing And Undrawn Credit Line At Pebblebrook Hotel Trust (PEB) Has Changed Its Investment Story
feed.summary: Pebblebrook Hotel Trust has refinanced its near-term debt by securing a new US$450 million term loan and extending its US$650 million revolving credit facility, which remains fully undrawn. This move significantly extends the company's debt maturity profile and enhances its financial flexibility, reducing balance sheet risk and potentially influencing its investment narrative positively. While improving liquidity, the refinancing does not address core operating risks like labor costs or competition, and investors should still consider the impact of potentially lower business travel in urban markets.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Discusses CVS Health (CVS) In Detail
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted CVS Health (CVS) as a promising investment, noting its strong position against competitors like Walgreens and Rite Aid and its diversified business model, including Aetna. Despite recent analyst price target adjustments due to Medicare/Medicaid reimbursement concerns, Cramer emphasized CVS's potential for growth. The article also briefly mentioned that some AI stocks might offer even greater returns with less downside risk.

feed.title: General Motors Company (GM) CEO Nailed It, Says Jim Cramer
feed.summary: Jim Cramer praised General Motors CEO Mary Barra for anticipating challenges in the electric vehicle (EV) market, contrasting GM's position with Stellantis's significant EV writedown. Analysts have reaffirmed "Buy" ratings for GM, with raised price targets, citing the company's strong price management and tariff navigation. Cramer highlighted Barra's foresight, suggesting GM "nailed it" in the EV landscape.

feed.title: Salesforce (CRM) is a Great Company, Says Jim Cramer
feed.summary: Jim Cramer considers Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) a "great company," despite its stock being down significantly over the past year. Analysts from Oppenheimer and Stifel reiterated Buy ratings with price targets around $300, citing platforms like Agentforce enabling competition in the AI era. However, Piper Sandler cut its price target due to concerns over AI-driven self-coding and seat-compression.

feed.title: Apple (AAPL)'s A Free Rider, Says Jim Cramer
feed.summary: Jim Cramer continues to favor Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL), viewing it as a long-term investment rather than a trading stock. Despite recent stock fluctuations, analysts maintain positive ratings, focusing on the potential of Apple Intelligence. Cramer highlighted Apple's strategic partnership with Google for AI, calling the company "the greatest free rider in history."

feed.title: Dallas Mavericks co-owner named CEO of Las Vegas Sands Casino
feed.summary: The Dallas Mavericks co-owner has been appointed as the new CEO of Las Vegas Sands Casino. Further details regarding this significant leadership change were not provided in the snippet. The article was published on February 15, 2026, at 9:15 AM CST.

feed.title: Agilent Technologies Stock: Quiet Rally, Hard Questions – Is The Lab Giant Still A Buy After Its Comeback?
feed.summary: Agilent Technologies has shown resilient performance, outperforming the broader market despite challenges in lab spending. The company's stock has rebounded, leading to questions about its future upside. Analysts are cautiously optimistic following recent earnings, with price targets suggesting further, albeit modest, gains.

feed.title: Public Storage moves its headquarters to North Texas from California
feed.summary: Public Storage, a prominent self-storage company, has announced its decision to relocate its headquarters from California to North Texas. This move signifies a shift in the company's operational base to a new region. The article, while brief, highlights this significant corporate relocation.

feed.title: Apple (AAPL)’s A Free Rider, Says Jim Cramer
feed.summary: Jim Cramer considers Apple (AAPL) a "free rider" due to its strategy of partnering with Google for AI development, thereby saving billions in investment. Despite a 4.5% stock increase over the past year, it's down 5.6% year-to-date. Analysts from Evercore ISI and Goldman Sachs maintain "Outperform" and "Buy" ratings respectively, with price targets of $330, citing the forthcoming Apple Intelligence AI platform and accelerating App Store spending.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Discusses Broadcom (AVGO) Stock
feed.summary: Jim Cramer has frequently praised Broadcom (AVGO) and its CEO, Hock Tan. Jefferies and UBS both maintain "Buy" ratings on the stock, citing its strong position in the AI sector and potential benefits from growing demand for tensor processing units. Despite its strong performance, Cramer believes the stock should be up even more, acknowledging NVIDIA's dominance but highlighting Broadcom's solid standing.

feed.title: Eaton (ETN) is Still Buyable, Says Jim Cramer
feed.summary: Jim Cramer remains bullish on Eaton Corporation (NYSE:ETN), recommending it as "still buyable" due to strong financial performance and analyst upgrades. RBC Capital and Morgan Stanley both raised their price targets for Eaton, citing strong fourth-quarter earnings and promising backlogs. Cramer specifically linked Eaton's prospects to the robust orders seen by electrical equipment provider Vertiv.

feed.title: Devon Energy Bets on Scale With Coterra Acquisition
feed.summary: Devon Energy announced an all-stock merger with Coterra Energy, creating a $58 billion energy company to enhance scale, diversification, and resilience in a consolidating oil and gas industry. The merger aims for operational efficiency and increased geographic reach, with a planned 31.5 cents per share dividend for the combined entity. Investors are watching Devon's upcoming Q4 2025 earnings report for management's confident tone regarding the merger and dividend future.

feed.title: KD SHAREHOLDER ALERT: Bronstein, Gewirtz and Grossman, LLC Announces that Kyndryl Holdings, Inc. Stockholders with Substantial Losses Have Opportunity to Lead Class Action Lawsuit!
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman, LLC has announced a class action lawsuit against Kyndryl Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:KD) and its officers. The lawsuit alleges that Kyndryl made false and misleading statements about its financial performance and internal controls between August 7, 2024, and February 9, 2026. Investors who suffered losses during this period are encouraged to join the lawsuit, with an April 13, 2026, deadline to seek appointment as lead plaintiff.

feed.title: NVIDIA (NVDA) Invented Its Category, Says Jim Cramer
feed.summary: Jim Cramer has consistently backed NVIDIA (NVDA), asserting that CEO Jensen Huang "invented the category" and remains unmatched in the field, especially concerning AI training and the software stack. Despite recent stock sluggishness, analysts like UBS and Goldman Sachs have raised price targets and maintained "Buy" ratings, citing a strong fiscal fourth quarter outlook and potential revenue beats. The article highlights Cramer's conviction that NVIDIA's lead in AI technology is unassailable.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD) Was Down Way Too Much, Says Jim Cramer
feed.summary: Jim Cramer commented that Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (NASDAQ:AMD)'s stock dipped "way too much" following its Q4 earnings report, despite strong revenue and guidance. While Goldman Sachs maintained a Neutral rating due to operating expenses concerns, UBS lowered its price target but kept a Buy rating. Cramer believes CEO Lisa Su "under promised so she could over deliver" and felt she presented well.

feed.title: Devon Energy Bets on Scale With Coterra Acquisition
feed.summary: Devon Energy announced an all-stock merger with Coterra Energy, set to create a $58 billion energy company focused on efficiency and diversification in a maturing U.S. shale industry. While the merger offers geographic expansion and a planned 31% dividend increase, investors are cautious about potential EPS dilution and are awaiting Devon's Q4 2025 earnings report for more clarity. Analysts have reacted positively, raising price targets, but market volatility is expected ahead of the earnings call.

feed.title: GSK plc (GSK) Q4 Results Topped Forecasts as Specialty Medicines Jump 17%
feed.summary: GSK plc announced strong fourth-quarter results, surpassing analyst estimates for both earnings per share and revenue, driven by a 17% growth in specialty medicines and a 43% increase in oncology sales. The company provided optimistic guidance for 2026, projecting sales growth of 3-5% and even higher growth in core operating profit and earnings per share. GSK attributes future growth to its oncology and respiratory medicines, as well as expanding pipeline assets.

feed.title: Ondas Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ:ONDS) Short Interest Update
feed.summary: Ondas Holdings Inc. (NASDAQ:ONDS) saw a significant 41.7% increase in short interest in January, reaching over 114 million shares, representing 27.5% of its float. Despite insider selling totaling $12.1 million, the company has received target upgrades from analysts based on new European defense orders and airport deployments. However, Ondas remains unprofitable and dilutive, with fundamental concerns about its execution and valuation.

feed.title: Analysts’ Opinions Are Mixed on These Technology Stocks: Interlink Electronics (LINK) and Paycom (PAYC)
feed.summary: This article summarizes analyst opinions on two technology stocks: Interlink Electronics (LINK) and Paycom (PAYC). Mark Murphy from J.P. Morgan maintained a Hold rating for Paycom with a $140 price target, while the consensus for Paycom is a Moderate Buy with a $148.21 target. The article also notes that TipRanks – Anthropic downgraded Paycom to Hold recently.

feed.title: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) Week in Review: 11% Stock Drop Despite 123% Growth
feed.summary: Super Micro Computer (SMCI) experienced an 11% stock drop this week despite reporting 123% revenue growth, primarily due to concerns over compressing gross margins. Goldman Sachs downgraded the stock, citing margin pressure and limited bargaining power with hyperscaler customers, while insider selling added to investor apprehension. Despite solid AI infrastructure demand, the market is demanding clearer proof of sustainable profitability from SMCI.

feed.title: Amcor (AMCR) Delivers Strong EPS and Synergy Gains After Berry Merger, Truist Reiterates Buy
feed.summary: Amcor (AMCR) reported strong adjusted earnings per share of $0.86, exceeding analyst forecasts, and achieved $55 million in synergies, surpassing its target. Truist Securities reiterated its Buy rating and a $60 price target for the company. The packaging manufacturer also saw revenue synergies reach over $100 million in annualized sales following the Berry business merger.

feed.title: Rivian, Magna International, And Akamai Are Among the Top 10 Large-Cap Gainers Last Week (Feb. 9-Feb. 13): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
feed.summary: This article highlights the top ten large-cap stock performers from February 9th to February 13th. Companies such as Magna International, Generac Holdings, and Rivian Automotive saw significant gains due to strong quarterly results, analyst upgrades, or strategic business announcements. The piece encourages investors to review if these high-performing stocks are part of their current portfolios.

feed.title: Uber Announces Rollout of Food Delivery Services to Seven Additional European Nations
feed.summary: Uber plans to expand its food delivery service to seven new European countries: Czechia, Greece, Romania, Austria, Denmark, Finland, and Norway, aiming for $1 billion in extra gross bookings over the next three years. This strategic expansion follows Uber's acquisition of Getir's delivery operations in Turkey and seeks to capitalize on consolidation within Europe's multibillion-euro food delivery market. The company is actively hiring operations managers and leveraging its existing European success to establish strong market positions and challenge incumbents.

feed.title: This Dividend ETF You Haven't Heard of Is Springing to Life
feed.summary: The article highlights the ProShares S&P MidCap 400 Dividend Aristocrats® ETF (REGL) as an under-the-radar option for dividend investors, especially with mid-cap stocks performing well. This ETF focuses on mid-cap companies that have consistently increased dividends for at least 15 years, offering a diversified, equal-weighted portfolio of 51 stocks. It suggests REGL could be a resilient choice for long-term investors, particularly appealing given its domestic sales focus amidst potential economic shifts.

feed.title: Southwest WiFi Upgrade And Board Changes Put Valuation And Risks In Focus
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines announced it will offer free Starlink WiFi and cabin upgrades across its fleet, alongside a reduction in its board size with two directors resigning. This initiative aims to enhance customer experience and competitiveness in the low-cost airline market, while the governance changes highlight a period of corporate restructuring. Investors should consider the impact on capital allocation, customer experience, and governance, as these moves may affect Southwest's market position and financial outlook.

feed.title: H.C. Wainwright Boosts Intellia (NTLA) Price Target After MAGNITUDE-2 Hold Lifted
feed.summary: H.C. Wainwright has raised its price target for Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA) to $25 from $15 and reiterated a Buy rating. This upgrade follows the lifting of a clinical hold on the company's MAGNITUDE-2 study. The revised protocol for the study now targets approximately 60 patients, an increase from 50, to mitigate risks and improve data understanding.

feed.title: Institutional investors in Rockwell Automation, Inc. (NYSE:ROK) see US$2.3b decrease in market cap last week, although long-term gains have benefitted them.
feed.summary: Institutional investors hold a significant 86% ownership in Rockwell Automation, Inc. (NYSE:ROK), making them highly influential. Despite a US$2.3 billion market cap decrease last week, these investors have benefited from a 36% one-year return. The company's top 22 shareholders collectively own 50% of the company, with The Vanguard Group, Inc. being the largest single shareholder.

feed.title: Veralto Corporation (NYSE:VLTO) Sees Large Decrease in Short Interest
feed.summary: Veralto Corporation (NYSE:VLTO) experienced a significant 19.8% decrease in short interest by the end of January, totaling 4,446,305 shares. The company reported strong Q4 earnings, beating EPS estimates, and also increased its quarterly dividend. Institutional investors hold a substantial 91.28% of the stock, while insiders have a smaller stake with a recent SVP share sale.

feed.title: Delta Air Lines, Inc. (NYSE:DAL) Sees Large Decrease in Short Interest
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) experienced a significant 19.8% decrease in short interest during January, totaling 15.94 million shares by the end of the month. Despite heavy insider selling amounting to $37.1 million over the last quarter, analysts maintain a bullish "Buy" rating with an average target price of $80.47. The company also surpassed its EPS estimates last quarter and provided strong guidance for FY2026.

feed.title: ArcelorMittal (MT) Reported Strong Q4 2025 Earnings Surprise, Expands Renewable Energy Capacity to 2.8GW by 2028
feed.summary: ArcelorMittal (MT) reported strong Q4 2025 earnings per share of $0.86, beating consensus estimates, though revenue was below expectations. The company plans to expand its renewable energy capacity to 2.8GW by 2028, expecting an additional $0.4 billion in EBITDA. ArcelorMittal is also growing its iron ore business in Liberia, aiming for 30 million tons capacity by extending operations until 2050.

feed.title: Amcor (AMCR) Delivers Strong EPS and Synergy Gains After Berry Merger, Truist Reiterates Buy
feed.summary: Amcor (AMCR) reported strong quarterly earnings, with adjusted EPS of $0.86, surpassing analyst expectations. The company also achieved $55 million in synergies after the Berry business merger, exceeding its target. Truist Securities reiterated its Buy rating and a $60 price target for Amcor, acknowledging its potential as an investment.

feed.title: Stellantis (STLA) Stock Rated Equalweight by Morgan Stanley After Strategy Concerns
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley downgraded Stellantis (STLA) from Overweight to Equalweight, citing concerns about its investments, product pipeline, market share, and financial indicators compared to peers. Despite these concerns, the firm acknowledged Stellantis's underperformance relative to other European automakers, its improving product selection, and the structural benefit of its exposure to US markets, which should offer protection from Chinese competition. The report suggests that while STLA has potential, certain AI stocks might offer greater upside and less risk.

feed.title: Stellantis (STLA) Stock Rated Equalweight by Morgan Stanley After Strategy Concerns
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley downgraded Stellantis N.V. (NYSE:STLA) from Overweight to Equalweight, citing concerns about the company lagging in investments, product pipeline, market share, and financial indicators compared to peers. Despite these worries, the firm noted Stellantis's underperformance compared to other European automakers and a gradually improving product selection, particularly in the U.S. market, which offers long-term benefits due to less exposure to Chinese competition.

feed.title: Chubb (CB) Strengthens Emerging Market Growth With Nubank Partnership, Citizens Maintains Market Outperform
feed.summary: Chubb Limited (CB) recently received a "Market Outperform" rating from Citizens with a $350 price target, highlighting its strong global presence and growth potential in emerging markets like Asia and Latin America. Key factors include its increased ownership in Huatai Insurance Group in China and a new digital life insurance service partnership with Nubank in Brazil, showcasing its focus on international expansion and digital innovation. The article suggests that while Chubb is a potential investment, certain AI stocks might offer greater upside and less downside risk.

feed.title: Is Public Storage (PSA) Offering Value After Recent Share Price Strength?
feed.summary: Public Storage (PSA) has seen recent share price strength, returning 16.2% year to date. A Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests the stock is undervalued by 34.6% compared to its estimated intrinsic value of $459.29 per share. Its current P/E of 33.25x is also slightly below Simply Wall St's "Fair Ratio" of 34.47x, further indicating it may be undervalued.

feed.title: Insiders At Targa Resources Sold US$35m In Stock, Alluding To Potential Weakness
feed.summary: Insiders at Targa Resources Corp. (NYSE:TRGP) have sold a significant amount of stock, totaling US$35 million over the past year, which could signal potential weakness to investors. The CEO & Director, Matthew Meloy, made the largest individual sale of US$9.5 million. While insider selling can have various explanations, the repeated sales by multiple insiders, including a recent US$498k dump by Executive VP Gerald Shrader, raise questions despite the company's profitability and high insider ownership.

feed.title: BlackRock & State Street Rejected Her ETF. It Just Returned 5X Its Category Average.
feed.summary: Perth Tolle's Freedom 100 Emerging Markets ETF (FRDM) has significantly outperformed its category, returning 99% over five years, five times that of the iShares MSCI Emerging Markets ETF. Despite being rejected by major players like BlackRock and State Street initially, FRDM's strategy of investing in countries with greater freedoms and excluding state-owned enterprises has led to its success and growth to $2.5 billion in assets under management.

feed.title: Papa Johns Is Angling for a Michelin Star—Could They Actually Get One?
feed.summary: Papa Johns is promoting its new pan pizza by humorously aiming for a Michelin Star, a prestigious culinary award. As part of this marketing stunt, the company is hosting a free, luxurious pop-up restaurant in New York City on February 19th, offering a chef-curated three-course meal of their pan pizzas with premium ingredients and soda pairings. While acknowledging it's a publicity move, the article suggests Papa Johns might be better suited for a Michelin Bib Gourmand, which recognizes high-quality food at great value.

feed.title: TimesSquare Capital Management LLC Buys 1,601,331 Shares of James Hardie Industries PLC. $JHX
feed.summary: TimesSquare Capital Management LLC significantly increased its stake in James Hardie Industries PLC (NYSE:JHX) by purchasing 1,601,331 shares in Q3, bringing its total holding to 1,601,731 shares valued at approximately $30.77 million. Other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in JHX, which has seen its revenue grow by 30.1% year-over-year. Analysts currently rate James Hardie Industries as a "Moderate Buy" with a consensus price target of $26.71.

feed.title: International Paper's (NYSE:IP) Dividend Will Be $0.4625
feed.summary: International Paper (NYSE:IP) announced a quarterly dividend of $0.4625 per share, payable on March 17th. While the dividend yields 3.8% and the stock has seen a 34% price increase in three months, concerns remain about the sustainability of the dividend due to the company not generating profits or free cash flow. Despite a promising forecast for earnings per share, analysts advise caution given the past decline in EPS and the current payout method.
